User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Maroma Experiences platform, which can be an individual booker, a school representative, a corporate contact, an admin, or a tour facilitator. This entity stores core contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication and login.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the user, for contact and notifications.

accountType.description: The type of account this user holds (e.g., 'Individual', 'School', 'Corporate', 'Admin', 'Facilitator').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

TourCategory.title: TourCategory
TourCategory.description: Defines categories for tours and workshops, allowing for better organization and filtering.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TourCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the tour category (e.g., 'Perfumery', 'Adventure', 'Culinary').

description.description: A brief description of what tours in this category entail.

Location.title: Location
Location.description: Stores geographical information for tour and workshop venues.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Location entity.

name.description: The name of the location or venue.

address.description: The street address of the location.

city.description: The city where the location is situated.

state.description: The state or province where the location is situated.

zipCode.description: The postal code of the location.

country.description: The country where the location is situated.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the location for mapping purposes.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the location for mapping purposes.

description.description: A detailed description of the location or venue.

Tour.title: Tour
Tour.description: Defines the template for a tour or workshop, including its general details, pricing, and media.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tour entity.

name.description: The name of the tour or workshop (e.g., 'Maroma Perfum Experience').

description.description: A comprehensive description of the tour, detailing activities and inclusions.

shortDescription.description: A brief, summary description of the tour for listings.

imageUrl.description: An array of URLs pointing to high-quality images related to the tour.

videoUrl.description: An array of URLs pointing to videos related to the tour.

pricePerPerson.description: The base price per person for this tour.

durationHours.description: The estimated duration of the tour in hours.

minimumGroupSize.description: The minimum number of attendees required for the tour to proceed, typically 8 as per app requirements.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the tour is currently active and available for booking.

categoryIds.description: References to TourCategory entities that this tour belongs to. (Relationship: TourCategory N:N Tour)

locationId.description: Reference to the Location where this tour primarily takes place. (Relationship: Location 1:N Tour)

facilitatorIds.description: References to User entities who are facilitators for this tour. (Relationship: User N:N Tour)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tour was created in the admin interface.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tour was last updated in the admin interface.

Media.title: Media
Media.description: Represents an image or video resource stored in the system's media library.

TourSchedule.title: TourSchedule
TourSchedule.description: Represents a specific scheduled instance of a Tour, including its exact date, time, and current booking status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TourSchedule entity.

tourId.description: Reference to the Tour entity this schedule belongs to. (Relationship: Tour 1:N TourSchedule)

startTime.description: The date and time when this scheduled tour begins.

endTime.description: The date and time when this scheduled tour is expected to end.

maxCapacity.description: The maximum number of participants allowed for this specific tour instance.

currentBookedSpaces.description: The current number of spaces booked for this tour instance.

status.description: The current status of this tour schedule (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'Full', 'Cancelled', 'Completed').

minimumGroupSizeMet.description: Indicates whether the minimum required group size for this tour schedule has been met.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this tour schedule was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this tour schedule was last updated.

AddOn.title: AddOn
AddOn.description: Represents an optional add-on that can be selected for corporate booking packages.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AddOn entity.

name.description: The name of the add-on (e.g., 'Catering Option A', 'Transportation').

description.description: A detailed description of the add-on.

price.description: The additional cost of this add-on.

type.description: The type of UI element used for selecting this add-on ('Dropdown', 'Checkbox').

isActive.description: Indicates whether the add-on is currently active and available.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Records a reservation made by a user for a specific tour schedule, supporting individual, school, and corporate bookings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this booking. (Relationship: User 1:N Booking)

tourScheduleId.description: Reference to the specific TourSchedule instance being booked. (Relationship: TourSchedule 1:N Booking)

numberOfAttendees.description: The total number of participants included in this booking.

totalPrice.description: The total price for this booking, including any add-ons.

bookingStatus.description: The current status of the booking (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Cancelled', 'Completed').

specialInstructions.description: Any special requests or instructions provided by the booker.

bookedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking was made.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking was last updated.

addOnIds.description: References to AddOn entities selected for this booking (primarily for 'Corporate' bookings). (Relationship: AddOn N:N Booking)

schoolName.description: The name of the school if the bookingType is 'School'.

corporateName.description: The name of the corporation if the bookingType is 'Corporate'.

contactPersonId.description: Reference to a User entity representing the primary contact for school or corporate bookings, if different from the userId who made the booking. (Relationship: User 1:N Booking)

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Records details of notifications sent to users, facilitators, or admins regarding booking-related events.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientUserId.description: Reference to the User entity who is the recipient of this notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

bookingId.description: Reference to the Booking entity that this notification is related to. (Relationship: Booking 1:N Notification)

messageContent.description: The full personalized message content of the notification.

generatedByAI.description: Indicates whether the message content was generated or assisted by AI (e.g., Gemini).

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was sent.

deliveryStatus.description: The delivery status of the notification (e.g., 'Pending', 'Sent', 'Failed', 'Read').

channels.description: An array of communication channels used to send this notification (e.g., 'Email', 'WhatsApp', 'Telegram').

definition.description: Stores core user profiles for individuals, schools, corporate contacts, and facilitators. Access is strictly path-based, meaning a user can only read or write their own profile data (where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document in this collection signifies that the `userId` corresponds to an administrator, granting elevated privileges across the application. Documents may contain minimal data, such as a timestamp or role activation status.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user designated as an administrator.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document in this collection signifies that the `userId` corresponds to a tour facilitator. Specific tour facilitation assignments are handled via denormalized fields in `Tour` and `TourSchedule` documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user designated as a facilitator.

definition.description: Global collection for defining and managing categories for tours and workshops. These are read-only for all authenticated users and managed (create/edit/delete) exclusively by administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tour category.

definition.description: Global collection storing geographical information for tour and workshop venues. These are read-only for all authenticated users and managed (create/edit/delete) exclusively by administrators.

params.name: locationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the location.

definition.description: Defines templates for tours and workshops. Documents are publicly readable only if `isActive` is true. Admin users can create, edit, and delete tours. Each tour document includes a `facilitatorIds` array for specific tour assignments and `tourOwnerId` for the admin who created it, enabling role-based access checks for specific tours.

params.name: tourId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tour or workshop.

params.name: mediaId
params.description: The unique identifier for the media item.

definition.description: Represents specific scheduled instances of tours. Publicly readable if `status` is 'Scheduled'. Managed by admins and assigned facilitators. Includes denormalized `facilitatorIds` (array of UIDs from the parent Tour) and `tourOwnerId` (UID of the admin who owns the parent Tour) for authorization independence.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tour schedule instance.

definition.description: Global collection for optional add-ons primarily used in corporate booking packages. These are read-only for users during the booking process and managed (create/edit/delete) exclusively by administrators.

params.name: addOnId
params.description: The unique identifier for the add-on.

definition.description: Records all reservations. Includes denormalized `userId` (the booker's UID), `tourOwnerId` (the UID of the admin who owns the tour), and `facilitatorIds` (array of UIDs from the associated Tour). This denormalization ensures Authorization Independence, allowing the booker to access their own booking, and facilitators/admins to access relevant bookings without `get()` calls to other collections. Supports efficient QAP-compliant queries by role.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the booking.

definition.description: Stores user-specific notifications. Access is strictly path-based, meaning a user can only read or manage their own notifications (where `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`). This provides strong segregation for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the recipient user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, QAP compliance, and DBAC principles to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All authorization logic aims to eliminate `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing crucial authorization context directly into the documents where it's needed. User-specific data is primarily segregated using path-based ownership where appropriate, while collaborative or globally visible data resides in top-level collections with denormalized fields for granular access.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **TourSchedule:** Each `TourSchedule` document will include a denormalized `facilitatorIds` array (copied from its parent `Tour`) and `tourOwnerId` (the UID of the admin who owns the parent `Tour`). This allows security rules to evaluate facilitator or admin access to a specific schedule directly from the `TourSchedule` document, without needing to `get()` the parent `Tour` document.
*   **Booking:** Each `Booking` document will contain the `userId` (the booker's UID), a denormalized `facilitatorIds` array (copied from the associated `Tour` via `TourSchedule`), and `tourOwnerId` (the admin who owns the associated `Tour`). This ensures that rules can determine access rights for the booker, assigned facilitators, and the tour's admin owner without expensive or complex `get()` operations on related `Tour` or `TourSchedule` documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   **Public Data:** Collections like `/tourCategories`, `/locations`, and `/addOns` are generally readable by authenticated users (and admins can write), with security rules validating this broad access. For `/tours` and `/tourSchedules`, the `isActive` (for Tour) and `status` (for TourSchedule) fields are used for explicit state modeling. Public queries will specify `where('isActive', '==', true)` or `where('status', '==', 'Scheduled')`, and the rules will validate that the `isActive` or `status` field indeed matches the public criteria for `read` operations, rather than the rules themselves performing filtering. This ensures that client-side queries for public data are inherently QAP-compliant.
*   **User-Owned Data:** `/users/{userId}/notifications` adheres to path-based ownership, making `list` operations secure as the `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. For `bookings`, a user can query their own bookings using `bookings.where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The security rules will then permit this `list` operation by verifying that `resource.data.userId` matches `request.auth.uid`.
*   **Facilitator & Admin Access:** Facilitators can list bookings or schedules specific to the tours they manage using queries like `where('facilitatorIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)`. Administrators have broad read/write access to all relevant collections, typically enforced by a global `is_admin` function checking the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection. The denormalized `facilitatorIds` in `TourSchedule` and `Booking` documents directly support these QAP-compliant queries.

**DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):**
*   Admin and Facilitator roles are managed via dedicated top-level collections: `/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_facilitator/{userId}`. The presence of a document in these collections grants the respective role, enabling simple and debuggable rule checks using `exists()`.

**Addressing the User's Issue:** The `Tour` entity includes an `isActive` boolean. When an admin creates a tour, it should default to `isActive: false`. General users will only see tours where `isActive: true`. This ensures that tours are not publicly visible until explicitly published by an admin, allowing the admin to see their newly created tour (as it doesn't need to be active for an admin to view it) without it appearing in public listings.
